subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus:
Water is pumped into a tank at a rate modeled by W(cid:11)t(cid:12) (cid:32) 2000e(cid:16)t2 20 liters per hour for 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 8, where t
is measured in hours. Water is removed from the tank at a rate modeled by R(cid:11)t(cid:12) liters per hour, where R is
differentiable and decreasing on 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 8. Selected values of R(cid:11)t(cid:12) are shown in the table above. At time
t (cid:32) 0, there are 50,000 liters of water in the tank.

parts.prompt: Estimate R(cid:132)(cid:11)2(cid:12). Show the work that leads to your answer. Indicate units of measure.

scoring_points.description: Correct estimate or setup with supporting work
Correct interpretation or answer with units

parts.prompt:
Use a left Riemann sum with the four subintervals indicated by the table to estimate the total amount of
water removed from the tank during the 8 hours. Is this an overestimate or an underestimate of the total
amount of water removed? Give a reason for your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct numerical answer or critical value

parts.prompt:
Use your answer from part (b) to find an estimate of the total amount of water in the tank, to the nearest
liter, at the end of 8 hours.

scoring_points.description: Correct identification of extremum or critical value

required_elements: Critical point analysis or sign change

scoring_points.description: Correct justification using first derivative test

parts.prompt:
For 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 8, is there a time t when the rate at which water is pumped into the tank is the same as the rate
at which water is removed from the tank? Explain why or why not.

scoring_points.description: Correct average value or rate integral
